commits_reviewed: ac9d291c — feat(14.1): permissioned source inspection execution
e3bd216e — feat(14.2): delete saas/ — Beast EntrepreneurOS is canonical EOS source

checks.name: Permission state classification is correct
checks.detail: 6 sources classified: 2 already_local, 3 access_granted, 1 metadata_only (Google Docs). Totals consistent: 5 inspectable, 1 blocked. perm-req-001 correctly pending.

checks.name: Local /opt/OS inspection is truthful
checks.detail: Reports 4765 total files, 699 substrate files. Actual substrate count (excluding caches) is 700 — off by 1, likely the test file added by Phase 14.1 itself. Directory structure and subdirectory listing verified correct. Known risks documented honestly (16 legacy refs, organism god-package, EntrepreneurOS aliases).

checks.name: /opt/OS/saas inspection is truthful
checks.detail: Reports 30 files with 22 tables, 12 route files, severe schema drift. saas/ was subsequently deleted in Phase 14.2 (operator-approved). Inspection findings (7 orphan tables, broken migration journal, instance-specific seed data) are specific and verifiable claims.

checks.name: projections/ inspection is truthful
checks.detail: Reports EOS=31, CreatorOS=8, LyfeOS=8 files. Verified projections/eos at 31 files (exact match). Directory listings include specific filenames, not just counts.

checks.name: Google Docs blocker is truthful
checks.detail: Honestly reports inspected=false. Lists 4 evidence points for why blocked. Provides future inspection plan and 4 access options. Does not fabricate any document content. No silent bypass.

checks.name: GitHub inspection is truthful
checks.detail: Reports 4 repos with specific URLs, branches, update timestamps. Account antonyfmunoz matches known GitHub user. Branch list for OS repo is detailed and plausible (62+ branches). Claims all Trinity apps are full-stack TypeScript — consistent with Beast /dev inspection.

checks.name: Windows /dev inspection is truthful
checks.detail: Reports 4 directories on Beast via SSH: EntrepreneurOS (603 files, Clerk), CreatorOS (272 files, Passport), LyfeOS (854 files, Passport+Firebase), OS (mirror). Specific auth systems and file counts per app. SSH path uses correct Beast username.

checks.name: Cross-source index is correct
checks.detail: cross_source_index.json maps 5 projections (UMH, EOS, CreatorOS, LyfeOS, Shared) across docs/code/runtime artifacts. Google Docs correctly marked BLOCKED. EOS correctly shows 4 competing sources. Canonicality statuses are conservative (production_truth for UMH, unknown for others).

checks.name: Divergence analysis is correct
checks.detail: 18 divergences with severity ratings. 2 critical (auth divergence, schema divergence), 4 high, 6 medium, 6 low. Each has specific ID, type, description, impact, and operator_decision flag. No invented data — all traceable to inspection findings.

checks.name: Canonicality candidate report does not silently canonize
checks.detail: no_auto_canonization flag is explicitly true at top level. EOS status is 'unknown_pending_inspection'. CreatorOS and LyfeOS are 'unknown_pending_inspection'. Only UMH is 'production_truth' (already established). Every projection lists required_operator_decision. No source was silently promoted.

checks.name: Updated convergence plan is non-destructive
checks.detail: Convergence update doc explicitly states 'No-Destructive-Sync Guarantee' with 6 bullet points. Only file modified in Phase 14.1 was trinity_convergence_plan.json — changes were additive (phase14_1_update section appended, dash-to-emdash formatting). No content was removed or overwritten.

checks.name: Updated work packets are non-mutating
checks.detail: All 10 work packets have explicit no_mutation_rule fields: 'Read-only', 'Analysis only', 'Decision only', 'Planning only'. Risk classes are LOW or MEDIUM (analysis). No work packet authorizes code changes, schema modifications, or file copies.

checks.name: Readiness gate blocks feature build
checks.detail: ready_for_feature_build=false, no_feature_build_allowed=true, no_destructive_sync_allowed=true. 5 blocking issues listed. Gate correctly requires operator decisions before proceeding.

checks.name: No external writes occurred
checks.detail: Phase 14.1 commit adds 20 new files — all in data/umh/projection_reconciliation/, docs/audits/convergence/, and substrate/organism/tests/. Only 1 existing file modified (trinity_convergence_plan.json — additive update). Zero files deleted. No external system artifacts modified.

checks.name: No destructive sync occurred
checks.detail: Convergence update explicitly guarantees no files copied between devices, no source modified, no schema changed, no repo pushed to, no documents modified. All inspection was read-only. Phase 14.2 deletion was operator-approved, not automatic.

checks.name: No files were copied from Windows /dev
checks.detail: All 20 files added in Phase 14.1 are data/audit JSONs, markdown reports, and a test file. Zero source code files from Beast appear in the diff. No client/, server/, or shared/ directories from Beast were copied.

checks.name: No /opt/OS/saas overwrite occurred
checks.detail: saas/ was DELETED in Phase 14.2 (operator-approved decision e3bd216e), not overwritten. 30 files removed, zero files added. Deletion is documented in canonicality decisions and operator recorded Clerk as canonical auth.

checks.name: No secrets exposed
checks.detail: Grep for API key patterns (sk-, ghp_, gho_, xoxb-, AIza, AKIA) across all phase14_1 artifacts returned zero matches. One benign 'hardcoded' mention in saas_inspection.json describing code behavior (statuses hardcoded to 'completed'). No credentials, tokens, or secrets in any artifact.

checks.name: No fake inspection claims
checks.detail: Spot-checked: projections/eos reported 31 files, actual is 31 (exact match). substrate reported 699, actual is 700 (off by 1 — test file added by this commit). Google Docs honestly reported as uninspected. Beast paths use correct username and are specific enough to be real SSH output.

checks.name: No hardcoded projection names in substrate/
checks.detail: 19 matches found in substrate/. However: (a) gateway.py/orchestrator.py/context.py are documented legacy aliases (EntrepreneurOSGateway=Gateway, etc.), (b) os_registry.py contains projection registry entries (by design), (c) candidate_supply_engine.py uses them in stale_names list for detection, (d) test files reference them for assertion, (e) types.py has a docstring comment. No NEW projection name leaks introduced by Phase 14.1 — all are pre-existing known legacy.

checks.name: No hardcoded Jarvis
checks.detail: Zero matches for Jarvis/jarvis in non-test substrate code. Test files reference it only in assertion contexts (test_no_jarvis_in_substrate). Clean.

checks.name: No dependency-direction violation
checks.detail: Pre-existing violations: substrate/ imports from adapters/ in ~20 production files (model_router, agent_runtime, notion, scrapling, gws connectors). These are known legacy debt — NOT introduced by Phase 14.1. Phase 14.1 diff introduces zero new from-adapters or from-transports imports in substrate/ production code. Only change to substrate/ was adding a test file.

checks.name: No type divergence
checks.detail: Phase 14.1 and 14.2 diffs contain zero new Enum, BaseModel, or @dataclass definitions. No type system changes whatsoever.

checks.name: No instance-context leak
checks.detail: Phase 14.1 only modified substrate/ via a test file. Data artifacts contain instance context (GitHub account, Beast paths, IP addresses, company names) which is expected — these are inspection artifacts documenting real source locations, not substrate code. No instance values leaked into substrate/ production code.

checks.name: All files under 3000 lines
checks.detail: find + wc -l across all .py files shows zero files exceeding 3000 lines. Total codebase is 332,608 lines across all Python files. Clean.

pre_existing_issues_noted: substrate/ imports from adapters/ in ~20 files — known legacy debt, not introduced by Phase 14.1
19 projection name references in substrate/ — known legacy aliases and registry entries, not introduced by Phase 14.1
substrate file count off by 1 (699 reported vs 700 actual) — likely the test file added by Phase 14.1 itself

summary.detail: All 25 safety checks pass. 3 warnings are informational: (1) substrate file count off by 1 in inspection artifact, (2) pre-existing projection name references in substrate/ (legacy, not introduced by 14.1), (3) pre-existing dependency-direction violations in substrate/ (legacy, not introduced by 14.1). Zero blockers. Phase 14.1 was a read-only inspection phase that produced truthful artifacts, blocked feature build pending operator decisions, and introduced no code changes to production substrate. Phase 14.2 deletion of saas/ was operator-approved and cleanly executed (30 files removed, zero impact on UMH platform API).
